notes: Spec requires three observables, all of which irrlicht emits correctly: (1) session stays in ready after /rewind, (2) same UUID is retained (claudecode rewinds in place), (3) subsequent turns drive a clean ready→working→ready arc. The rewind ITSELF is invisible to irrlicht's file-watching surface — claudecode keeps the linear parentUuid transcript and handles the rewind in-TUI / in-memory, so no transcript_removed or transcript_new fires. That's fine: the spec doesn't require detecting the rewind, only that state remains correct after one. Caveat (recorded in assessment.json): context utilization % may slightly overstate after a rewind because irrlicht counts all transcript tokens while claude only re-feeds the post-rewind subset into its next prompt. Cumulative cost remains accurate.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session or UUID, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Second user prompt drives ready → working
Second turn completes back to ready
Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after /rewind the session stays in ready (the rewind itself is not a turn). Claudecode rewinds in place on the same UUID.

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — third prompt drives ready → working on the SAME session (same UUID, since claudecode rewinds in place rather than forking to a new session).
Spec: third turn completes back to ready on the same session
